content.problemStatement:
Chef is participating in an ICPC regional contest, in which there is a total of NNN problems (numbered 111 through NNN) with varying difficulties. For each valid iii, the iii-th easiest problem is problem AiA_iAi‚Äã.

After a team solves a problem, a balloon with a colour representing that problem is tied next to their desk. Chef is fond of colours in **VIBGYOR**, which are representative of the problems with numbers 111 through 777. The remaining problems have their own representative colours too.

Find the minimum number of problems which Chef's team needs to solve in order to get all the balloons for problems 111 through 777 (and possibly some other balloons too) tied next to their desk, if you know that Chef's team knows the difficulties of all problems and solves the problems in increasing order of difficulty.
constraints: 1‚â§Ai‚â§N1 \leq A_i \leq N1‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the minimum number of problems Chef's team needs to solve.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Since there are a total of 777 problems, Chef's team will have to solve all of them.
Example case 2: Problems 111 through 777 appear among the first 888 problems.
Example case 3: Problems 111 through 777 again appear among the first 888 problems.
